semantic.role: Mock LLM client for testing and prompt validation. Implements the LLMClient interface to simulate LLM responses, and includes built-in logic to detect rule violations (e.g., infra facade import violations) in evaluation prompts.
responsibilities: Provide a mock implementation of LLMClient for testing without external API calls.
Parse raw rule strings (e.g., from architectural rule blocks) into structured objects (id, severity, body) for analysis.
Detect violations of import rules (e.g., direct imports from infra submodules) in prompts by scanning for forbidden patterns and path regexes.
Optionally flag other architectural constraints (e.g., 'must not import' patterns) based on configured rules.
outOfScope: Does not perform actual LLM inference or API calls to any provider.
Does not define or enforce all possible architectural rules; only provides detection for a subset implemented in its logic.
Does not handle production configuration, authentication, or external service integration.
invariants: Must not itself violate the infra-facade-imports rule; its imports are limited to stable facade modules (../base.js, ../../prompt-policy.js).
Detection logic must remain adaptable to evolving architectural rules and rule block formats.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a test-friendly LLM client that also validates prompts for rule adherence, enabling E2E testing of architectural constraints.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Recent fix addressed ESLint errors, E2E prompt injection vulnerabilities, and better-sqlite3 compatibility; likely hardened the MockClient's prompt parsing and injection detection.

publicSurface.description: Mock implementation of the LLMClient interface for testing. Simulates LLM responses and validates prompts against architectural rule violations, such as direct imports from infra submodules when a facade exists.
